slides.title: The fiber that *grabs your cholesterol*.
slides.sub: It's called beta-glucan — the soluble fiber in oats. It binds cholesterol in your gut and can help lower LDL.

slides.title: Beta-glucan forms a gel
slides.body: In your gut, *beta-glucan* turns into a gel that traps cholesterol and carries it out before it's absorbed.

slides.title: Lower LDL over weeks
slides.body: Eating oats daily can *modestly lower LDL* — the cholesterol tied to clogged arteries.

slides.title: Aim for 3g a day
slides.body: Studies use about *3g of beta-glucan daily* — roughly one big bowl of oats or oat bran.

slides.title: Slow, even fuel
slides.body: That same gel slows digestion, so oats give *steady energy* instead of a sugar spike and crash.

slides.title: Make it a default
slides.body: Whole or steel-cut oats beat instant. Add berries and nuts — skip the *sugar-loaded packets*.

slides.title: Eat for a *healthier heart*.
slides.body: Vibe Health logs your meals and habits into one daily Health Score — so heart-smart swaps like oats actually stick.

caption:
There's a fiber in oats that literally grabs cholesterol on its way out. 🥣

It's beta-glucan: about 3g a day can modestly lower LDL, and the same gel slows digestion for steady energy. Skip the sugary instant packets and go whole or steel-cut. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health folds swaps like this into one daily Health Score so they stick → link in bio.
youtube_title: The Fiber In Oats That Lowers Cholesterol
